study: Habitat effects on intra-species variation in functional morphology in the Pearl River, China (2018)
authors: Shuai F, Li X, et al. (珠江水产研究所 李新辉团队)
key_findings: 6 fish species examined for intra-specific functional morphology
Regional factors (elevation, water temperature) STRONGER than local factors
Flow velocity, river fractals, coefficients of fluvial facies affect morphology
Morphological traits: body shape, fin position, mouth position
our_advantages: Pearl River: 6 species. Yangtze: 430 species scale
Pearl River: morphology only. Yangtze: 7 trait categories
Pearl River: ecological gradients. Yangtze: + taxonomic validation + fishing ban impact
Pearl River: one river system. Yangtze: multiple water bodies (reservoir/lake/river/estuary)
Same institution (李新辉 = Pearl River; 刘凯 = Yangtze) — potential for comparative paper!
comparative_paper_idea: Comparative intraspecific trait variation between Pearl River and Yangtze River fish communities: Implications for large river ecosystem management
